title: Lecture Two Twist-Angle Berry Curvature Context
summary: This equation context records how Witten turns the twist angles into adiabatic coordinates, reads off the Berry connection on the twist-angle torus, and integrates its curvature to recover the Hall-response level.
assumptions: The finite-volume ground state remains nondegenerate over the twist-angle torus.
Only the one-derivative adiabatic term is needed to read off the Berry connection.
regime: Many-body adiabatic transport on the torus of spatial holonomies.
scope: Equation-level context for the many-body part of the Lecture Two equivalence proof.

source_anchors.notes: The adiabatic twist-angle route to the many-body Chern number.

representation: Equation context for the adiabatic Berry-connection computation on the twist-angle torus.

math_expressions.notes: The effective action reduced to the twist-angle adiabatic term.
Berry connection on the torus of twist angles.
Constant Berry curvature fixed by the Hall-response level.
The many-body Chern number equals the macroscopic Hall-response coefficient.

symbols.meaning: twist angles or background holonomies along the two spatial cycles
Berry curvature over the twist-angle torus
many-body Chern number defined from the twist-angle Berry curvature

retrieval_hints: Open this when the answer must reconstruct the Berry-phase argument step by step.
